mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, creating a favorable environment for positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >60% of observations or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and supply-side capacity, which improves corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a hit rate below 45% across rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and broader demand destruction, which reduces construction and related sector earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing start declines are followed by positive equity returns in >50% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rates indicate a healthy labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, yielding positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative returns in >55% of triggered months or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and generates negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 200 is associated with positive equity returns in >50% of observations, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when inflation expectations are anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic growth that increases corporate earnings potential, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or the hit rate falls below 40%.
